title: digitalis lanata subsp. leucophaea
intro: Digitalis lanata subsp. leucophaea, also known as white foxglove, is a subspecies of the Digitalis lanata plant, a biennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It contains digitalis glycosides, a group of compounds that have been used for centuries in the treatment of heart conditions, particularly atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The medicinal properties of white foxglove have been used to slow the heart rate, increase the force of heart contractions, and improve cardiac output, making it a valuable herbal remedy for cardiovascular health. White foxglove is typically prepared as a tincture or extract, which can be used to treat a range of heart conditions, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional herbal medicine.
